  "findings": [
    "Shared neutral configuration causing AFCI nuisance tripping",
    "Improperly paired multiwire branch circuit (MWBC) neutrals on single-pole AFCI breakers"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the affected circuits immediately",
    "Trace and isolate the shared neutral connections",
    "Group and tie the associated hot and neutral conductors together",
    "Install a 2-pole AFCI breaker or separate the neutrals to dedicated circuits"
  ],
  "rationale": "An AFCI breaker detects current imbalances between the hot and neutral conductors. A shared neutral between two separate single-pole AFCI breakers causes an imbalance that results in immediate nuisance tripping. The circuits must be de-energized and the neutral isolation verified before troubleshooting or re-energizing can safely continue.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 210.4",
    "NEC 210.12",
    "NEC 200.4"
  ]
}
